# Figure description: Gamma XIII.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/geophysical sections/Gamma XIII.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **geophysical cross-section (профиль / profile)**, specifically a combined **gravity and magnetic profile** plotted along a survey traverse line. It is a typical Soviet-era regional geophysical interpretation sheet showing observed and theoretical field curves alongside a schematic geological-density model of the subsurface. The figure is designated as **Профиль XIII** (Profile XIII), drawn at a scale consistent with 1:200,000 regional survey work. It corresponds to **Чертёж №110, Лист 7** (Drawing No. 110, Sheet 7), dated **1984**.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers **Profile XIII**, one of a series of regional geophysical traverses from the West-Tengiz (Западно-Тенгизская) geophysical party survey, referenced to **Report No. 36572** (Smolianova et al., 1983–1984), northern Kazakhstan, map sheets M-42 / N-42. The horizontal axis spans approximately **0 to 900+ km** in profile-distance units (likely in kilometres or survey stations), divided into numbered segments (stations 1 through approximately 7 are visible along the bottom). The vertical axes reflect **elevation/depth in metres** and **gravity/magnetic anomaly values in mGal or nT**.

---

## Key Content

The figure is divided into **three horizontal panels**:

### Upper Panel — Topographic and Gravity/Magnetic Curves
- Shows two curves plotted against a horizontal distance axis:
  - One curve (upper, more irregular) likely represents the **observed magnetic field (ΔT or Za)** or **Bouguer gravity anomaly**, showing a prominent **peaked anomaly** in the central portion of the profile (around station 400–500), reaching approximately **1000–1100 units** (mGal or nT), with a secondary smaller peak to the right (~700–800 station range).
  - A second smoother curve below it likely represents the **theoretical (modelled) curve** or a regional field.
- A **circled cross symbol (⊗)** appears at two points along the upper curve, likely indicating **borehole/drill intersection points** or control points (скважины).
- The left portion of the profile (stations 0–300) shows relatively flat, low values (~400 units), rising sharply in the central section and falling again toward the right.
- Scale on the left vertical axis appears to read: **ΔZ, γ / ΔG мГл** with values marked at approximately 400, 600, 800, 1000 (upper scale) and 2, 4, 6, 8, 10 (right-side scale), suggesting dual gravity/magnetic axes.

### Middle Panel — Residual Anomaly Curve
- Plotted below a **zero datum line**, this panel shows the **residual (local) gravity or magnetic anomaly** after regional trend removal.
- Values range from approximately **0 to –400 to –500 units** (negative anomalies dominating the left and central sections), with a notable **negative trough** around stations 100–300 (reaching approximately –400 to –500), and a positive/near-zero segment in the central-right section.
- Two **⊗ symbols** are again visible, marking borehole positions.
- A scale of **мм З** (mm, scale for magnetic or gravity residual) is shown on the left.

### Lower Panel — Density/Geological Model with Anomaly Values
- This is a schematic **geological-density cross-section** divided into numbered blocks (segments 1–7 along the bottom).
- Each block is annotated with **density contrast values (γ)** in **г/см³ (g/cm³)**:
  - Segment 1: **+0.03 г/см³**, **–0.35 г/см³** (γO₃ label)
  - Segment 2: **0.00 г/см³**, **+0.5 г/см³**, **γO₂**, **–0.07 г/см³**
  - Segment 3/4: **+0.04 г/см³**, **0**, **РК₄б+**, **+0.15 г/см³**, **γO₂**
  - Segment 5/6: **+0.005 г/см³**, **–0.09 г/см³**, **γδO₂**, **+0.03 г/см³**
  - Segment 7: **0**, **+0.04 г/см³**
- The geological units are labelled with Russian stratigraphic/lithological symbols: **γO₂**, **γO₃**, **γδO₂**, **РК₄б+**, suggesting **Ordovician granites (γO₂, γO₃)**, **gabbroic/dioritic intrusions (γδO₂)**, and possibly **Cretaceous or Paleozoic sedimentary cover (РК₄б+)**.
- A thin undulating line at the top of this panel likely represents the **density interface or modelled basement surface**.

- **Top right:** `Чертёж №110 Лист 7` (Drawing No. 110, Sheet 7)
- **Top left date:** `1984г.` (year 1984)
